chapter_title.ar: باب الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ مِنَ الْحَرَائِرِ وَ الْإِمَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – The gathering between two sisters, from the free ones and the slaves

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/196)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/196)
text: رَجُلٍ طَلَّقَ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى أَ يَتَزَوَّجُ أُخْتَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ تَضَعَ قَالَ لَا يَتَزَوَّجُهَا حَتَّى يَخْلُوَ أَجَلُهَا .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who divorced his wife and she was pregnant, can he marry her sister before she places (gives birth)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He cannot marry her until her term is cleared’.
en.sarwar: 8. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Zurarah who has said the following: “About the case of a man who divorces his wife and she is pregnant; if he can marry her sister before she gives birth, he (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘He must not marry her until her time is complete.’”
